description: Civilian unemployment rate: share of the labor force that is jobless and actively seeking work, seasonally adjusted (percent).
ICE BofA US High Yield OAS: option-adjusted spread of below-investment-grade corporates over comparable Treasuries (percent).
St. Louis Fed Financial Stress Index: composite of Treasury, corporate, equity, and funding-market stress indicators (index).
10-year minus 3-month Treasury constant-maturity spread: benchmark yield-curve slope and recession-risk gauge (percent).
10-year TIPS real yield: market yield on inflation-indexed U.S. Treasury securities, investment basis (percent).
10-year breakeven inflation rate: market-implied average inflation expected over the next 10 years from Treasury and TIPS yields (percent).
S&P 500 death-cross signal: ratio of 50-day to 200-day moving average; below 1.0 indicates bearish crossover (ratio).
10-year Treasury constant-maturity yield: market yield on U.S. Treasury securities at 10-year maturity, investment basis (percent).
Nominal broad U.S. dollar index: trade-weighted value of the dollar against a broad set of foreign currencies (index, Jan 2006=100).
Reserve balances with Federal Reserve Banks: week-average deposits of depository institutions held at the Fed (millions of U.S. dollars).
Overnight reverse repo facility usage: Treasury securities sold by the Federal Reserve in temporary open-market operations (billions of U.S. dollars).
Secured Overnight Financing Rate: broad measure of the cost of borrowing cash overnight collateralized by Treasury securities (percent).
